name: 60s Pop Culture
description: Music, politics, and cultural moments of the 1960s

questions.q: In what year was President John F. Kennedy assassinated?

source.notes: November 22, 1963, in Dallas, Texas. Lee Harvey Oswald was charged.

questions.q: Which Apollo mission first landed humans on the Moon in 1969?

source.notes: Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins. Landed July 20, 1969.

questions.q: Which country won the 1966 FIFA World Cup, held on home soil?

source.notes: England's only World Cup victory. Defeated West Germany 4-2 at Wembley.

questions.q: Which band released the 'Surrealistic Pillow' album (featuring 'White Rabbit') in 1967?
choices: Big Brother and the Holding Company

source.notes: Fronted by Grace Slick. 'Somebody to Love' also on this album.

questions.q: At the 1965 Newport Folk Festival, which artist controversially performed with an electric guitar?

source.notes: July 25, 1965. Audience included a booing reaction, marking his shift to electric rock.

questions.q: Which legendary guitarist, who died in 1970, had Cherokee ancestry and set his Stratocaster on fire at Monterey Pop?

source.notes: Left-handed player who played right-handed guitars strung upside down.

questions.q: Roger Daltrey was the lead singer of which English rock band?

source.notes: Also includes Pete Townshend, John Entwistle, and Keith Moon.

questions.q: Who was the wild drummer of The Who, known for destroying drum kits onstage?

source.notes: Died of overdose in 1978 at age 32. Nicknamed 'Moon the Loon.'

questions.q: Jimmy Page left which 1960s band to form Led Zeppelin?

source.notes: Eric Clapton, Jeff Beck, and Jimmy Page all spent time in The Yardbirds.

questions.q: In 1969, which Canadian singer-songwriter wrote the song 'Woodstock'?

source.notes: Ironically, Mitchell didn't attend Woodstock. Song made famous by CSNY.

questions.q: Who sang the signature version of 'My Way' in 1969, a song that became his anthem?

source.notes: English lyrics by Paul Anka, based on a French song. Sid Vicious also famously covered it.

questions.q: What was The Beatles' first #1 hit in the United States?
choices: I Want to Hold Your Hand

source.original_a: I Want to Hold Your Hand
source.notes: Reached #1 on Feb 1, 1964. Sparked the British Invasion.

questions.q: What was the Rolling Stones' first #1 hit in the UK, often shortened to '(I Can't Get No) ___'?

source.notes: Released 1965. Written by Keith Richards and Mick Jagger.

questions.q: John Lennon and Yoko Ono released which anti-war anthem in 1969?

source.notes: Recorded during their 'Bed-In' for peace in Montreal.

questions.q: Who composed the famously sneaky theme music for the 1963 film 'The Pink Panther'?

source.notes: Also composed 'Moon River' and the theme for 'Peter Gunn.'

questions.q: Which band released 'Wild Thing' in 1966?

source.notes: English band. The song's original was by The Wild Ones in 1965, but Troggs version was the hit.

questions.q: Who wrote the song 'Suzanne,' covered by many 60s artists?

source.notes: First recorded by Judy Collins in 1966. Cohen released his own version in 1967.

questions.q: Who composed the primary songs for The Velvet Underground?

source.notes: Wrote 'Sweet Jane,' 'Walk on the Wild Side,' and 'Pale Blue Eyes.' Died 2013.

questions.q: In 1968, George Harrison's first solo album, often titled 'Wonderwall Music,' was the soundtrack to what?

source.notes: Featured Indian classical music. Name inspired Oasis's 1995 song 'Wonderwall.'

questions.q: Which soulful Motown girl group released 'Stop! In the Name of Love' in 1965?

source.notes: Diana Ross, Florence Ballard, Mary Wilson. One of 12 #1 hits for the group.
